justification: As the Monsun operator, A is the more useful brief. A frames the 2026 hiring environment around concrete regulatory pressures an operator must manage this quarter — the EU Pay Transparency Directive forcing visible widelki, PIP's crackdown on misclassified umowa zlecenie, and the new seniority rules retroactively granting 26-day leave eligibility. Those are real compliance levers I can act on. A also flags the toxic 1.5%-of-sales tip-out mechanic specifically, which is a recruiting/retention issue I'd want to avoid replicating. B is broader on sourcing (36 domains vs 15, including TipTip's 17.88 PLN cashless-tip data and a PIE shadow-economy figure) and is internally consistent, but its core thesis leans on 'pod stolem' cash envelopes — not something a legitimate Swietojanska operator can build a hiring strategy around. B wins source_diversity; A wins coverage and actionability; specificity and specialist set are TIE. Overall A.
